creator: rm@gnu.org
text:
Sorry for the delay, I have issues testing gcc and objc.
I had two FreeBSD/x86 systems - however one broke and I had to replace it, it still runs FreeBSD but amd64 (this took me some time and excuses my delays). I don't know if the issue would apply to a different architecture, but would be interesting to test.

However, both on the workstation and laptop I have another issue now: gcc6 and gcc7 there seems to be no supplied objc runtime! I don't know if it is a "choice" of FreeBSD to cater to the other libobjc2 package or not. It used to have its own.

However, using gcc7 (which could reproduce the issue, but on a different file) I am able to *compile* the whole gnustep framework. Thus I think the compiler issue is fixed indeed, no more ICE.

as a note, on 10.4 the gcc meta-port still pulls me in gcc6.
